item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Woodward admits in the first line that he's terrible about keeping records. The logs are informal and intended for his own personal reference. These are necessary shortcuts to keep the narrative concise and within the boundaries of the format, but I tried to at least ensure everything in the text follows an internal logic. If it's still too much of a stretch for you, fair enough!
  • Cursory research says that "suss" actually started being used in the 1930s, but that's still an anachronism so I've changed it. Good catch!
  • The legfish was already dead when it was removed, so it wouldn't have chewed through anything. Woodward didn't mention what he did with it afterward because he's the worst at keeping records. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is still so good, body horror is my Favorite thing i just love it

Who would win?

The entire crew of a sea vessel

OR

Some shark flesh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has really great pictures and a really good concept, however, I feel this leans pretty heavily on its ''found letter'' which really does not feel like a letter from a professional military doctor in 1922 I mean the phrase ''sussed'' did not come around until the 1960s and began as British police jargon if I recall. Likewise, the medical terminology seems spotty there's a reference to ''pills'' but what doctor, even back then wouldn't note at least the type of medication they were giving to their patient? Also, there's no mention of the doctor either trying to refrigerate the leg for further study or incinerate it as an obvious health hazard does this skip somehow make military doctors clumsier such things?
Why not have a note about attempting to contain or dispose of it and then note something like ''it ate through X container!o.0''

All in all, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some great body horror and a great 4th episode of this strange-yet-spectacular SPC-inspired anthology. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should be fixed now. In my rush to meet the contest deadline, I had forgotten to localize the images, and the URL broke today. Thanks for the heads-up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like the files have been removed. Not quite sure what's going on there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not the only one for whom the images are broken, am I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, regardless, this is amazing. The Center/Centre is actually a legitimate venue for horror that the Foundation needs more of: benthic horror. Ia Ia Sharkthulhu, etc.

I somehow didn't upvote this, but I've rectified that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ew. Terrible. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. This is the first I've heard of this meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …is this based entirely on the "Teeth became shark" meme that was going around the site a few years ago?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, I think, the best application of the Shark Punching Center to the SCP format.

God freaking dammit all to hell. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor shark got punched so hard he became an infection in another universe. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, this is some really good body horror!

+1

item.title: Secondary Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Shark Fighter -- toughest man in the sea!
Shark Fighter -- with his wavy blond hair flowing so freely!

(His only goal is a shark casserole)
(His only creed is to make sharks bleed)
(He is not mean, he's just getting even)
(He will not die 'til all sharks cry)

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback! I've made some tweaks that should fix the article's appearance on mobile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Hey" the shark mouth that had grown on the SPC agent's fist whispered. "Stop hitting yourself."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this was gross in the best ways. Feels like a series 2 scip too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, somebody better call SPC.

This is a genuinely horrifying skip, it hooked me from the first paragraph and that's not easy to do. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gods this is horrifying. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is revolting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus Christ, this is some scary business!

All jokes apart, this one is sooooo good! Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I'm concerned, this is the canonical answer to the question, "where did all the sharks go?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep thinking I'm going to get completely jaded to body-horror, and then some spine-crawling shit like this comes along to keep me up at night. A+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goodness that's a frightening end to something that started out silly. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is some solid body horror! My one real criticism is that, at least on mobile, the images seem very clustered up near the top, and mess with the containment procedures.

Easy +1 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The final DoomCon entry for Team JAWESOME!!! Something is coming to the surface…..

The alternate title for this one is Fish Fingers.

Special thanks to…

  • Captain Kirby
    • SCP-3123 — Insult To Injury
  • The Great Hippo
    • SCP-2571 — Cragglewood Park
  • Jacob Conwell
    • SCP-3060 — Sleep Paralysis
  • daveyoufool
    • SCP-2219 — PORRIDGE
  • CadaverCommander
    • The Shape of a Gun
Image credits
– hide block

I don't think I used all of these but I don't feel like sorting through them so everyone gets credit!!!

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Modern_surgery,_general_and_operative_(1914)_(14780245204).jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grey_nurse_shark_2.jpg

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chemical_burn_injury..jpg
